title: Affirm Holdings Q3 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Affirm Holdings Q3 2021 Earnings Call - Key Excerpts. Financial Performance: Affirm exceeded expectations with GMV growth of 83% to $2.3 billion and revenue growth of 67% to $231 million. CEO Max Levchin stated: We more than doubled our active merchant count year over year to nearly 12,000, demonstrating strong business expansion. Strategic Achievements: The company announced significant progress with Shopify, noting that more than 10,000 merchants have gone live with Shop Pay installments in just April and early May. Additionally, Affirm acquired Returnly to enhance returns processing capabilities. Travel Category Resurgence: Travel and ticketing emerged as a major growth driver, with the category nearly tripled compared to the previous quarter. By April, travel represented 11% of total GMV, up from just 2% in September. Technology Advantage: Management emphasized Affirm's competitive moat, stating the company uses proprietary machine learning to personalize offers for consumers and manage risk more efficiently than competitors, enabling flexible payment solutions across varying price points. Credit Performance: The allowance for credit losses declined to a record low of 5.2% of loans held for investment, reflecting stronger-than-expected repayment history and improved credit outcomes during economic recovery.
